Home / Services / Stump Grinding & Removal

Stump Grinding & Removal

Remove unsightly tree stumps and reclaim your yard.

After a tree is removed, the stump can be an eyesore and a potential hazard. Tree Masters, Inc. offers professional stump grinding and removal services to completely eliminate tree stumps from your property. Our specialized equipment can grind stumps of any size, leaving you with a clean and level surface. We can also remove the stump grindings and backfill the area with topsoil, preparing it for new landscaping.

Stump grinding is a cost-effective and efficient way to remove tree stumps. Our stump grinders use high-speed blades to grind the stump into small wood chips. The depth of the grind can be adjusted to accommodate your specific needs. After the stump is ground, we can remove the grindings and backfill the area with topsoil. This creates a smooth and even surface that is ready for planting or other landscaping projects.

Tree Masters, Inc. is committed to providing safe and efficient stump grinding services. Our team is trained to operate our equipment safely and responsibly. We take precautions to protect your property and minimize disruption to your landscape. We also offer stump removal services for situations where grinding is not feasible.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Assessment - We evaluate the stump and its surroundings to determine the best grinding method.
  2. 02 Step 2: Grinding - Our skilled team grinds the stump to the desired depth.
  3. 03 Step 3: Cleanup - We remove the grindings and backfill the area with topsoil (if requested).
  4. 04 Step 4: Restoration - We level the area and prepare it for new landscaping.

Benefits

  • Elimination of unsightly stumps
  • Prevention of hazards
  • Improved aesthetics
  • Increased usable space
  • Preparation for new landscaping

Frequently Asked Questions

How long does stump grinding take?

The time it takes to grind a stump depends on its size and the type of wood. Most stumps can be ground in a few hours.

Will stump grinding damage my lawn?

We take precautions to minimize damage to your lawn during stump grinding. We use mats to protect the grass and carefully control the direction of the grindings.

Can I plant grass or a new tree where the stump was?

Yes, you can plant grass or a new tree where the stump was. We recommend removing the stump grindings and backfilling the area with topsoil to provide a healthy growing medium.
